steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

study_inadequacy.evidence: CT scan of abdomen and pelvis w/IV contrast which showed a distended tip of appendix upto 12mm without fat stranding, distended gallbladder containing multiple stones with subtle fat stranding. These findings were however quoted as 'very limited' given his body habitus.
study_inadequacy.summary_note: The outside CT was explicitly self-declared 'very limited' due to body habitus, leaving both the appendix (12 mm tip, no fat stranding) and gallbladder findings unresolved — the clinical question is still open.

alarm_detect.summary: The outside CT was explicitly characterized as 'very limited' due to morbid obesity, rendering its findings (distended appendix tip to 12 mm, gallbladder stones with subtle fat stranding) non-diagnostic and leaving the underlying cause of acute abdominal pain unanswered.

alarm_resolve.reason: The physician explicitly recognizes the outside CT was 'very limited' due to body habitus and orders a repeat high-quality CT abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast at the current institution specifically to resolve the uncharacterized appendiceal and gallbladder findings, directly targeting the study inadequacy flagged.
